commit.message:
test(w71): feature gate + capability reporting tests (~40 tests) (#601)

## Summary

Add comprehensive feature gate boundary tests enforcing the **\"no green
by omission\"** invariant — when optional features
(git/content/walk/halstead) are unavailable, the code must not silently
succeed or produce empty results.

### \crates/tokmd-analysis/tests/feature_gates_w71.rs\ (26 tests)
- **Git feature gate**: risk/git/identity/deep preset git requirements,
\git=false\ override
- **Content feature gate**: health/architecture/deep content-dependent
enrichers
- **Walk feature gate**: supply/security walk-dependent enrichers
- **Receipt baseline**: receipt preset works without any optional
features
- **Health without git**: health preset does not require git
- **Risk degradation**: risk preset degrades gracefully with \git=false\
- **Capability reporting**: preset plan resolution,
\preset_plan_for_name\, \
eeds_files\ consistency
- **Receipt metadata**: schema_version, tool name, mode, warnings,
status
- **Warning contract**: \DisabledFeature\ keywords, git/walk/content
mentions
- **Grid completeness**: all 11 presets covered, deep superset of
receipt

### \crates/tokmd/tests/feature_gates_w71.rs\ (14 tests)
- **Help text**: preset values, format option, git flag visibility
- **JSON envelope**: warnings array, status field, schema_version, tool
name
- **Graceful degradation**: invalid preset fails, \--no-git\
suppression, receipt always succeeds
- **Cross-preset validation**: all presets produce valid JSON with
required fields
- **Receipt exclusion**: receipt JSON excludes optional sections (git,
entropy, assets)

commit.message:
test(w71): BDD scenario tests for user workflows (~30 tests) (#599)

## Summary

Adds 38 BDD-style scenario tests in
\crates/tokmd/tests/bdd_scenarios_w71.rs\ following Given/When/Then
naming conventions.

### Test Coverage

| # | Scenario | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|
| 1-3 | Language detection | Rust project, multi-language, empty dir |
| 4-5 | Module & exclude | Exclude patterns, module depth grouping |
| 6 | Export CSV | All files listed in CSV |
| 7 | Diff | Two receipts produce diff output |
| 8 | Exclude flag | CLI --exclude applies during scan |
| 9 | Unicode | Unicode filenames normalized |
| 10-11 | Ignore rules | Hidden files excluded, .gitignore respected |
| 12-15 | JSON validity | Valid JSON + schema_version for
lang/module/export/analyze |
| 16-19 | Determinism | Identical output on repeated runs |
| 20-22 | check-ignore | Explains why files are excluded |
| 23-32 | Misc commands | Badge, init, tools, completions, version, TSV,
no-ignore-vcs |

### Approach

- All tests use hermetic temp directories with \.git\ markers
- Deterministic: no flaky assertions, no time-dependent checks
- BDD naming: \given_X_when_Y_then_Z\
- Uses existing \common::fixture_root()\ for shared fixtures

commit.message:
fix: break circular dependency in HTTP interceptors

tokenInterceptor and errorInterceptor injected I18nService directly,
creating a circular dependency when I18nService constructor triggered
HTTP requests (/api/v1/languages). The inject() call during I18nService
construction caused the request to fail silently, leaving the progress
bar pendingRequests counter permanently incremented.

tokenInterceptor: read language from localStorage directly instead of
injecting I18nService. errorInterceptor: lazily resolve I18nService
via Injector inside catchError callback (runs async, after construction).

commit.message:
Add ByteArrayOutputKind enum with ViewType and LargeOffsets options

Replaces the boolean UseViewTypes with a ByteArrayOutputKind enum on
ParquetReadOptions, with UseViewTypes kept as a backwards-compatible shim.

ByteArrayOutputKind values:
- Default: StringType/BinaryType with int32 offsets (unchanged behavior)
- ViewType: StringViewType/BinaryViewType (formerly UseViewTypes=true)
- LargeOffsets: LargeStringType/LargeBinaryType with int64 offsets,
  removing the 2 GB per-column limit with otherwise identical decode path

Changes:
- ParquetReadOptions.cs: adds ByteArrayOutputKind enum; UseViewTypes becomes
  a computed init-only property over ByteArrayOutput
- ArrowSchemaConverter: ApplyOutputKind replaces ApplyViewTypes, handles all
  three enum values in a single switch expression
- ColumnBuildState: _largeOffsetsBuffer (NativeBuffer<long>) for LargeOffsets
  mode; constructor takes ByteArrayOutputKind; AddByteArrayValues and
  CommitByteArrayData write to the correct buffer based on mode
- ArrowArrayBuilder: BuildLargeStringArray, BuildLargeBinaryArray,
  BuildLargeVarBinaryArray, BuildNullableLargeVarBinaryArray,
  BuildDenseLargeVarBinaryArray added; Build/BuildDense switches extended;
  ArrowArrayFactory handles LargeStringArray/LargeBinaryArray
- ColumnChunkReader: detects LargeStringType/LargeBinaryType from field type
- 5 new LargeOffsets tests + EW_ReadAll_LargeOffsets benchmark variant

commit.message:
Investigate 10->5 (2n->n diagonal) and compute closed-form volumes

- 10->5: vol=14.475, sym=20 (D5xZ2), not equilateral. Self-dual case.
  The 2n->n diagonal: 4->2(D8) -> 6->3(Ih) -> 8->4(D4xZ2) -> 10->5(D5xZ2)
- Closed-form volumes computed symbolically:
  10->3: V = 4(143*sqrt(5)+320) / (5*(3*sqrt(5)+7)^(3/2))
  12->3: V = 2(9+7*sqrt(2)) * sqrt((5-2*sqrt(2))/17)
- Add 2n->n diagonal table and closed-form volume table to README

commit.message:
fix: progress bar never completing on page load

Remove provideClientHydration() — without SSR its TransferCacheInterceptorFn
absorbed HTTP requests (e.g. /api/v1/languages) without completing the
observable, causing the progress interceptor's start() to never get a
matching complete() via finalize().

Also add a 10s safety timeout to ProgressBarService as defense-in-depth:
if pendingRequests stays positive for 10 seconds, force-complete.

commit.message:
Enable NativeAOT publishing for SkillValidator (#221)

* Enable NativeAOT publishing for SkillValidator

Migrate all serialization to source-generated, AOT-compatible patterns:

- Add PublishAot to csproj and Vecc.YamlDotNet.Analyzers.StaticGenerator package
- Create SkillValidatorJsonContext with source-generated serializers for all types
- Create SkillValidatorYamlContext with static YAML deserialization for eval config
  and frontmatter parsing
- Change AgentEvent.Data from Dictionary<string, object?> to
  Dictionary<string, JsonNode?> for AOT-safe serialization
- Replace anonymous type in Reporter.cs with named ResultsOutput record
- Fix JsonStringEnumConverter to generic AOT-safe JsonStringEnumConverter<T>
- Move ConsolidateData to Models.cs for JSON context accessibility
- Switch EvalSchema and SkillDiscovery to StaticDeserializerBuilder
- Add RawFrontmatter model for strongly-typed YAML frontmatter parsing
- Update all JsonSerializer call sites to use context-based overloads

The published binary is a single 14.6 MB native executable.
All 198 existing tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add NativeAOT publish matrix and daily scheduled builds to CI

- Single build job with matrix for linux-x64, linux-arm64, win-x64, win-arm64, osx-arm64
- Each matrix entry: build, test, NativeAOT publish, pack NuGet, upload tarball
- collect-packages job: builds RID-agnostic package, merges with per-RID packages
  into a single skill-validator-nupkgs artifact
- Add daily schedule trigger (08:00 UTC) with 90-day retention
- Keep existing path-based triggers for push/PR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add NativeAOT publish matrix and daily scheduled builds to CI

- Single build job with matrix for linux-x64, linux-arm64, win-x64, win-arm64, osx-arm64
- Each matrix entry: build, test, NativeAOT publish, pack NuGet, upload binary
- Upload publish directory directly (Actions wraps in zip automatically)
- collect-packages job: builds RID-agnostic package, merges with per-RID packages
  into a single skill-validator-nupkgs artifact
- Add daily schedule trigger (08:00 UTC) with 90-day retention
- Keep existing path-based triggers for push/PR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky DelayClampedToRemainingBudget test

Increase totalTimeoutMs from 200ms to 2000ms. The tight budget was
causing the CTS to fire before the retry attempt on slower CI runners
(macOS arm64). The test still validates that the 60s base delay gets
clamped — the 5s wall-time assertion is the real check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cancel in-progress workflow runs on new push

Add concurrency group so that pushing a new commit to a PR
automatically cancels any still-running workflow for that ref.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix per-RID NuGet pack with --use-current-runtime

The pack step was producing the same agnostic package for every RID.
Adding --use-current-runtime ensures each matrix entry produces a
RID-specific nupkg that gets collected into the combined artifact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant publish step; pack already produces native binary

dotnet pack --use-current-runtime already runs publish internally,
producing the native AOT binary in artifacts/publish/. The separate
dotnet publish step was redundant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename nupkgs artifact to nuget-bundled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant PublishTrimmed; implied by PublishAot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky ExponentialBackoff_DelaysIncrease test

Increase baseDelayMs from 50ms to 200ms so that OS scheduling jitter
on slow CI runners (macOS arm64) doesn't dominate the measured gaps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove per-RID publish directory artifacts; nupkgs suffice

NuGet packages are zip files containing the native binaries, so the
separate skill-validator-<rid> publish folder uploads are redundant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove collect-packages job; produce agnostic nupkg from linux-x64

Pack the RID-agnostic package from the linux-x64 matrix entry instead
of a separate downstream job, eliminating the bundle step entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename artifact names from nuget-* to skill-validator-*

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename agnostic artifact to skill-validator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify NuGet artifact uploads; remove rm -rf and collect-packages

Use precise glob patterns with the stable package name to distinguish
RID-specific from agnostic nupkgs. The agnostic upload uses a negation
pattern to exclude the RID-specific package on linux-x64.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract retention-days and agnostic RID into workflow env vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add nightly release job using gh release

On scheduled runs, downloads all nupkg artifacts and publishes them
as a rolling 'skill-validator-nightly' pre-release on the repo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore PropertyNameCaseInsensitive for JSON deserialization

The original MCPServerDef deserialization used PropertyNameCaseInsensitive
which was dropped when switching to source-generated context. Add it back
to the JsonSourceGenerationOptions to preserve flexible property casing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
